sources.label: SCMP - CATL deploys humanoid robots at scale on the factory floor (Spirit AI Xiaomo, Dec 2025 - background, no new development since Jun 6)
PRNewswire - HyperStrong/CATL 10-year, 200 GWh agreement (Nov 2025 - confirmed not new)

verdicts.rankRationale: No robotics-specific news since Jun 6; the Xiaomo humanoid deployment proof is from Dec 2025 and already priced into the thesis, and the rank already improved from #47 to #39 since yesterday's hold. A $268B mega-cap battery maker offers near-zero robotics purity, so no residual alpha case for moving again on a +1.65% drift day.
verdicts.bull: CATL is the most credible at-scale proof point that humanoids work in real manufacturing (Xiaomo at >99% connection success, ~3x manual daily workload), and it powers the robots' batteries - a quiet picks-and-shovels angle on embodied AI.
verdicts.neutral: All recent flow (Citi estimate raise Jun 5, Nanjing Bus pact Jun 4, charge-swap network Jun 3) is battery-business news, not robotics; the stock is up only 5.8% YTD, so neither crowding nor a sell-off changes the asymmetry on this page.
verdicts.bear: Robotics is a rounding error in a $268B battery business; the humanoid factory story is a cost-efficiency tool, not a revenue line, and the listing exists mainly as ecosystem proof rather than an investable robotics exposure.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Expansion of humanoid deployment beyond the Luoyang Zhongzhou base or a disclosed robotics/embodied-AI battery supply program (e.g., dedicated humanoid battery line or Spirit AI stake) that converts proof-of-concept into a revenue vector.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Robotics narrative stays immaterial to earnings while EV-battery pricing pressure or China demand softness drags the whole stock, leaving zero page-specific alpha.
